title: SpaceX Opened at $150: What Else Happened on Day 1?
author: Jack Delaney
description: SpaceX completed its IPO on June 12, 2026, pricing at $135 and opening to the public at $150 per share, closing at $160.95 for a 19.2% first-day gain. The company achieved a $2.1 trillion market cap, making it one of the world's most valuable companies. However, competing space stocks declined significantly on the news, with investors potentially rotating out of other space sector companies to buy SpaceX shares.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong IPO performance with 19.2% first-day gain from IPO price, opened at $150 vs $135 IPO price, achieved $2.1 trillion market cap placing it in top 10 most valuable companies globally, and continued to rise post-IPO.
Dropped more than 10% on SpaceX's first day of trading, likely due to investor rotation from competing space stocks to SpaceX and market digestion of SpaceX's dominant market position.
Shares dipped 15.5% on SpaceX's IPO day, indicating investor preference for SpaceX over competing satellite communications companies.
Shares fell nearly 11% on SpaceX's first trading day despite potential 2% stake ownership, suggesting broader sector volatility and investor rotation toward SpaceX.
Mentioned as context that SpaceX's $2.1 trillion market cap exceeded Tesla's $1.5 trillion market cap, but no direct impact on Tesla reported in the article.

title: Stock Market Today, June 12: EchoStar Falls as SpaceX-Linked Rally Meets DISH DBS Payment Risk
author: Eric Trie
description: EchoStar (SATS) fell 10.96% on June 12 as profit-taking followed recent SpaceX-related gains and credit concerns emerged after its DISH DBS subsidiary missed a $183 million interest payment due June 1. The company is relying on AT&T spectrum sale proceeds to resolve the payment issue and ease liquidity pressures amid declining pay-TV subscribers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 10.96% due to missed interest payment by DISH DBS subsidiary ($183M), triggering default with 30-day grace period. Company faces liquidity concerns, declining pay-TV subscribers, and year-over-year revenue declines. Recovery depends on timing of AT&T spectrum sale proceeds.
Closed down 5.19% alongside mixed sentiment in the telecom services sector, but no specific company-related news mentioned in the article.
Finished up 0.46% with mixed sentiment reflected across communications names, but no specific company-related news mentioned in the article.

title: Stock Market Today: SpaceX Jumps To $175, Rocks Space Rivals
author: Piero Cingari
description: SpaceX debuted on Nasdaq at $175, up 30% from its $135 IPO price in the world's largest IPO at $1.75 trillion valuation. The listing triggered a rotation in the space sector, with competitors experiencing significant declines. The broader market was mixed, with the S&P 500 up 0.2%, Dow up 0.5%, and Nasdaq down 0.1%, as geopolitical concerns about U.S.-Iran negotiations and falling oil prices influenced trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 30% from IPO price of $135 to $175 by midday, marking the world's largest IPO ever at $1.75 trillion valuation
Plunged 14.6%, the worst performer in Russell 1000, as investors rotated capital toward SpaceX
Tumbled 10.1% as traders booked profits ahead of SpaceX listing
Sank 17% due to competitive pressure from SpaceX's market entry
Fell 16% as a SpaceX parts supplier proxy amid sector rotation
Dropped 15% in the broader space sector selloff
Surged 7.3% as lithium prices rebounded and Citi reiterated bullish outlook
Surged 7.3% as lithium prices rebounded and Citi reiterated bullish outlook
Jumped 6.2% following Bank of America double-upgrade to Buy from Underperform
Fell 6.7% despite beating earnings estimates, due to CFO exit and ongoing CEO search concerns
Dropped 6.9% following Q1 earnings disappointment and tariff commentary
Slid 6.8% amid analyst price-target cuts and valuation concerns

title: SpaceX Starts Trading At $150, Before Rising To $165: 'Liftoff', Says Elon Musk-Led Company
author: Erica Kollmann
description: SpaceX (SPCX) completed the largest public offering in history, opening at $150 per share on June 13, 2026, representing an 11% pop over its $135 IPO price and valuing the company at approximately $1.96 trillion. The stock subsequently climbed to $165. The IPO raised $75 billion with demand exceeding $250 billion. However, the opening price fell below pre-market expectations of $174, suggesting institutional price discovery tempered early speculative enthusiasm. Space-related competitors experienced significant declines following the debut.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong IPO performance with opening price 11% above IPO price, subsequent climb to $165, massive demand ($250B for $75B offering), and qualification for early large-cap index inclusion providing structural support.
Stock fell over 10% following SpaceX's IPO debut, indicating investor capital rotation away from smaller space-related competitors toward the larger SpaceX offering.
Stock declined over 10% on SpaceX IPO day, reflecting competitive pressure and capital reallocation from smaller space companies to SpaceX.
Stock declined over 10% on SpaceX IPO day, reflecting competitive pressure and capital reallocation from smaller space companies to SpaceX.
Stock dropped 9% following SpaceX's market debut, indicating investor preference shift toward the newly public SpaceX over other space-related equities.
Stock fell 12% on SpaceX IPO day, experiencing the largest decline among space-related competitors due to capital rotation and competitive concerns.

title: EchoStar Stock Drops After Yesterday's Surge: What's Happening?
author: Evette Mitkov
description: EchoStar (SATS) shares fell 6.41% on Friday, reversing Thursday's rally driven by excitement over its SpaceX stake ahead of SpaceX's IPO. The pullback appears to be profit-taking by short-term traders rather than a fundamental shift. EchoStar's underlying catalyst—its $8.5 billion SpaceX stock position and partnership with SpaceX for Starlink Direct to Cell—remains intact. The stock trades below key moving averages but maintains longer-term support levels.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the stock experienced a 6.41% decline on Friday, the article emphasizes that the underlying catalyst (SpaceX stake and partnership) remains intact. The pullback is characterized as normal profit-taking after an event-driven rally rather than a fundamental deterioration. The longer-term technical picture remains constructive with support levels in place.
